libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No required environment variables or system properties. Meaningful tests should focus on the core artifact surface that does not require a live Kafka broker, such as Kafka binder configuration properties, Spring Boot property binding, BinderHeaderMapper, KafkaTopicUtils/BindingUtils behavior, and provisioner error-path behavior with unreachable or stubbed admin settings.
risks: Full end-to-end Spring Cloud Stream Kafka binding with real produce/consume traffic normally requires a Kafka broker, but that is not necessary before meaningful coverage of the requested core artifact is reachable.

The documented application dependency for normal users is spring-cloud-stream-binder-kafka or spring-cloud-starter-stream-kafka, while this issue targets the lower-level spring-cloud-stream-binder-kafka-core artifact; adding the non-core binder would shift coverage away from the requested artifact.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved core artifact brings its compile-time Spring Cloud Stream, Spring Boot Kafka, Spring Integration Kafka, and Kafka client dependencies transitively, and its own public surface includes configuration/property, header-mapping, topic utility, and provisioning classes that can be exercised without adding a broker, optional starter, or test dependency.
